intro: Stachycarpus ladei, also known as the Indian staff tree, is a medicinal plant belonging to the Staphylocarpaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Stachycarpus ladei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stachycarpus ladei is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or extract, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, arthritis, and digestive issues.
